metadata.description: Floyd-Warshall: All nodes find the shortest path. Dynamic programming. O(V³). The code is extremely concise. Here's an example of how you might implement this in Python using dynamic programming to solve the all-pairs shortest path problem (APSP) with Floyd-Warshall algorithm: ```python def floyd_warshall(graph): V = len(graph) dist = [[float('inf')] * V for _ in range(V)] # Initialize the distance matrix for i in range(V): for j in range(V): if graph[i][j] != 0: dist[i][j] = graph[i][j] elif i == j: dist[i][j] = 0 # Floyd-Warshall algorithm for k in range(V): for i in range(V): for j in range(V): if dist[i][k] + dist[k][j] < dist[i][j]: dist[i][j] = dist[i][k] + dist[k][j] return dist # Example graph represented as an adjacency matrix graph = [ [0, 3, float('inf'), 7], [8, 0, 2, float('inf')], [5, float('inf'), 0, 1], [2, float('inf'), float('inf'), 0] ] shortest_paths = floyd_warshall(graph) print(shortest_paths) ``` This Python code implements the Floyd-Warshall algorithm to find the shortest paths between all pairs of nodes in a graph. The `graph` is represented as an adjacency matrix where `float('inf')` indicates no direct edge between two nodes.

data_pointers.title: Floyd-Warshall
